title: Elemental sulfur-based mixotrophic denitrifying membrane bioreactor
abstract: The present invention is concerned with the development of a system for the simultaneous nitrate and chromate reduction by combining the advantages of sulfur-based autotrophic denitrification heterotrophic denitrification and membranebioreactor (MBR) technologies. The invention has been especially applied to eliminate the disadvantages of heterotrophic denitrification and sulfur-based autotrophic denitrification with the mixotrophic process which it is a method for the reduction of nitrate and Cr (VI). Through the invention, the produced sulfate in the sulfur-based autotrophic denitrification below the limit values and the alkalinity requirement was eliminate by means of the alkalinity produced in the heterotrophic denitrification process. In addition, thanks to the membrane (8) used in the system, microorganisms with slow growth rate keeps in the bioreactor (7). At the same time, the wash away of small particle size materials from the system was prevented.
claims: Claims It is a treatment system for the removal of nitrate and Cr (VI) from drinking water and includes methanol feed tank (1 ), feed tank (2) in which the treated water is stored, feed pump (3), feed controller (4), level sensor (5), sulfur feed tank (6), bioreactor (7), membrane (8) , stirrer (9), pressure sensor (10), suction pump (1 1 ), effluent tank (12) and the computerized monitoring and control system (13). The invention is a treatment system in claim 1 , characterized in : to make less methanol consumption than the required amount of methanol of system and weekly sulfur feed (6) to reduce the remaining nitrate, in order to reduce the influent total nitrate amount of the mixotrophic bioreactor (7), The membrane (8) in the bioreactor (7) keeps microorganisms in the bioreactor (7), Simultaneous usage of methanol and sulfur from methanol feed tank (1 ) and sulfur feed tank (6), respectively.
